hook.scenario: Your startup's app shows an 'average' salary of 20 LPA, but 9 out of 10 devs make 5 LPA while the founder takes 1.5 Cr. This is why standard 'averages' lie, and why you need median and dispersion to see the real picture.

mental_model.intuition: Mean is the balancing point, median is the middle guy, mode is the popular choice, and dispersion is how wildly the crowd is scattered.
mental_model.description: Statistics summarize large datasets into single numbers. Central tendency tells you where the middle is, and dispersion (variance/standard deviation) tells you how reliable that middle is.

formula.statement: Mean = Sum/N. Median = Middle value. Mode = Most frequent. Variance = Average of squared deviations. SD = √Variance.
formula.explain: Standard Deviation (SD) measures volatility. A high SD means data points are spread far from the mean.

shortcuts.title: Shift and Scale
shortcuts.trick: Add 'k' to all numbers -> mean/median/mode shift by 'k', but Standard Deviation stays exactly the same. Multiply by 'k' -> EVERYTHING gets multiplied by 'k'.
shortcuts.example: Original SD = 5. Add 10 to all values -> New SD is still 5.

shortcuts.title: The Empirical Formula
shortcuts.trick: Mode ≈ 3 × Median - 2 × Mean (for moderately skewed distributions).

common_mistakes.title: Forgetting to Sort for Median
common_mistakes.explanation: You cannot pick the middle element of an unsorted array. Always sort in ascending order first.

common_mistakes.title: Variance vs. Standard Deviation
common_mistakes.explanation: Variance is in squared units. If you are asked for standard deviation, don't forget to take the square root at the end!

recall_questions.q: If the standard deviation of a dataset is 4, what is the variance?
If you add 5 to every number in a dataset, what happens to its standard deviation?

oa_questions.question: The mean of 5 observations is 10. If a new observation 22 is added, what is the new mean?
oa_questions.approach: Total was 50. New total is 72. New mean = 72 / 6 = 12.

oa_questions.question: The standard deviation of 10 values is 3. If each value is multiplied by 4, what is the new variance?
